---
title: "Postcard from Ljubljana"
date: "2026-06-22"
---

![](../images/Ljubljana2.jpg)

Back from six days in Ljubljana. Very hot -- too hot to want to pause long to take carefully composed photos! So this is just a quick glipse of the long riverside walk that runs through the centre of the city. 

We were there mostly to hang out with the Digital Nomad Daughter, and the heat restricted the amount of exploration we felt like doing. But from what we saw, Ljubljana seems a delightful place, a very liveable city with a markedly relaxed feel (perhaps not unconnected with the fact that Slovenia has not enouraged the kind of mass tourism which spoils so many cities). By the cathedral, a stunning market with large stall after large stall with a wonderful range of produce, with some days small stalls too where locals bring in what they have grown and made (a table of berries and jams here, a table of honey there). Along the river, well-frequented café after café, with such friendly welcomes at least in the ones we tried. Cyclists everywhere, but few cars in the centre.

A paradigm of a very pleasing inner city environment, with the river and green spaces and lots of trees, without any sense of being a museum piece -- some of the modern buildings scattered around are, shall we say, not so successful, but the patchwork gives a sense of life. We would very much like to return in spring, DV. 

Another glimpse, walking down to the river. And note the beautiful paving again! -- sigh, such a shaming contrast to the visual mess of central Cambridge ...

![](../images/Ljubljana3.jpg)
